The six month call. This is the value your opinion call. Jared, listen to this. Six month call. Value your opinion call. That's what we call this. Look, in this call you need to create a reason to call this person whereby they can do you a personal favor that has nothing to do with your business that you know them for. People want to help each other. I want you to tap into this. Okay, again, this is not related to the product or service or your company but something this buyer knows about, something this person is an authority in, something this person has an ability to help you with that nobody else can do for you. For instance, when I first moved to Miami, I called a client. Actually, he wasn't a client, he was a prospect that I've been trying to get for 20 years. I called him up and said, Rick, the reason I'm calling is I know you're heavily committed to and work with the Boys and Girls Club of America. I wanted to get some feedback about how I might get involved with that charity. I really value your input. I'm looking at a couple of the charities to get involved with but I heard the Boys and Girls Club stands heads above others. What do you do? Oh my gosh, Grant, let's do lunch. I'd love to meet with you. I'd love to tell you about that. It makes him, puts him in a position, a legitimate position to help me out. People want to help others. I'll tell you another example. A very successful ball player who also invest in real estate. I reached out to and I reached out and said, look, the reason I'm calling is to see if I can make 10 minutes this week where I could show you an investment, not for you to invest in, but I value your opinion and would like your input on how you view this. I got a meeting with him. Okay. I got a meeting with somebody. If I mentioned your name, you'd be like, no, you can't get a meeting like that. That was a cold call. That's what you call a cold call. It's what I call follow up. Another situation where a customer loves watches and you sell insurance. I'm just hybridting a situation right now. I'm giving you a reason. Hey, the reason I'm calling is I wanted to get your opinion of the best watch for me to start with. Now, keep in mind, you're selling insurance. You know, he's a watch collector. Find something he's an expert on and ask them their opinion. It's called the valued opinion call. It's done. It's six months.
